title: Maintenance rotation + review-cadence/staleness live + translation-ready hand-off to ewing-info-translations
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task sustains the published guide: a named maintenance rotation, a running review cadence with re-sign-off on changes, a live staleness fail-safe, and a translation-ready hand-off to ewing-info-translations (which carries its own per-language HIGH-tier review). Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Stand up the maintenance rotation and live review-cadence/staleness fail-safe, and hand reviewed, translation-ready content to ewing-info-translations, tracking PII-free outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Named maintenance rotation; review cadence running (sources re-verified; oncologist re-sign-off on changes, advocate too if wording changed); staleness fail-safe verified live (no page served past validUntil)
Outcomes tracked qualitatively (usefulness, reach), not engagement metrics; no PII
Reviewed, translation-ready content handed to ewing-info-translations (which carries its own per-language HIGH-tier review)
